steps.title: Compare the evidence
steps.content: The text presents Mieszko II Lambert as a documented ruler who reigned from 1025 to 1031 CE. Siemomysł is treated differently: he is less well documented, is said to have ruled in the 10th century, and has disputed historical actuality.

steps.title: Reject unsupported comparisons
steps.content: Choice B reverses the evidence by claiming the chronicle gives more support for Siemomysł, even though the text says its documentation of him relies on oral tradition. Choice C gets the chronology backward because Siemomysł is associated with the 10th century, before Mieszko II Lambert's rule. Choice D invents orally transmitted stories about Mieszko II Lambert. The real contrast is certainty about existence. The answer is A.
